title: T-058 — Add warehouse permission strings to enum (§3, §6.6, §14)
prompt:
# T-058 — Add warehouse permission strings to enum

## Goal
Append a delimited `WAREHOUSE` permission group to `packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts` covering the 12 canonical RBAC strings declared by PRD `docs/prd/05-WAREHOUSE-PRD.md` (§3, §6.6, §14). Settings/Auth owns the enum file; this task only contributes a new namespaced section under the `warehouse.*` prefix. Export `ALL_WAREHOUSE_PERMISSIONS` so the ESLint enum-lock guard (02-settings T-130) recognizes the module group.

## Implementation contract
- File: `packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts` — append entries to `Permission` const object with JSDoc citing the PRD anchor. Do NOT remove or rename any existing string.
- File: `packages/rbac/src/__tests__/permissions.test.ts` — extend the suite to assert presence, regex `^[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+$`, uniqueness, and `ALL_WAREHOUSE_PERMISSIONS` typed `readonly Permission[]` with length === 12.
- File: `CODEOWNERS` — verify `/packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts @monopilot/architect` line exists; if missing, add it (already present today).

## Permission strings (12)
- `warehouse.grn.create`
- `warehouse.lp.split`
- `warehouse.lp.merge`
- `warehouse.lp.block`
- `warehouse.lp.move`
- `warehouse.qa_status.change`
- `warehouse.adjustment.approve`
- `warehouse.fefo.override`
- `warehouse.scanner_lock.force_release`
- `warehouse.settings.edit`
- `warehouse.dashboard.view`
- `warehouse.inventory_value.view`

## Files in scope
- `packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts` [modify]
- `packages/rbac/src/__tests__/permissions.test.ts` [modify]
- `CODEOWNERS` [modify if missing entry]

## Acceptance criteria (Given-When-Then)
- AC1 — Given the enum file is imported, when the consumer reads the new keys, then all 12 strings listed above are present exactly once.
- AC2 — Given each new permission string is matched against regex `^[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+$`, when tested, then every string passes and zero duplicates exist across the union of `Object.values(Permission)`.
- AC3 — Given the module export, when imported, then `ALL_WAREHOUSE_PERMISSIONS` is exported as typed `readonly Permission[]` with length === 12.
- AC4 — Given `CODEOWNERS` is read, when checked, then `packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts` is architect-only (line `/packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts @monopilot/architect` present).

## Test strategy (RED first, vitest)
1. Extend `packages/rbac/src/__tests__/permissions.test.ts` with the four ACs above. Run RED:
   `pnpm --filter @monopilot/web vitest run packages/rbac/src/__tests__/permissions.test.ts`
   — must fail before any enum change.
2. Implement minimum changes in `permissions.enum.ts`.
3. Re-run vitest — must pass.
4. Run `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm lint` in the changed package; both must pass.

## Risk red lines
- Do not weaken the regex `^[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+$` or introduce a 4-segment / matrix permission model.
- Do not introduce a parallel permission model (no per-module `*PermissionEnum` in another file).
- Do not remove or rename existing strings (Settings/Foundation strings stay intact).
- Do not edit permission strings owned by other modules.
- Do not commit secrets or vault keys.

## Closeout evidence required
- changed_files (with line counts)
- exact RED→GREEN test commands and outputs
- acceptance_criteria_status (one row per AC)
- deviations_from_prd (or "none")
- git_status

pipeline_inputs.description: Add 12 `warehouse.*` permission strings to packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts and export ALL_WAREHOUSE_PERMISSIONS; closes RBAC enum-lock gap flagged in _meta/audits/2026-05-14-prd-vs-tasks-coverage-gaps.md for module 05-warehouse.
pipeline_inputs.details: Strings (count=12): ['warehouse.grn.create', 'warehouse.lp.split', 'warehouse.lp.merge', 'warehouse.lp.block', 'warehouse.lp.move', 'warehouse.qa_status.change', 'warehouse.adjustment.approve', 'warehouse.fefo.override', 'warehouse.scanner_lock.force_release', 'warehouse.settings.edit', 'warehouse.dashboard.view', 'warehouse.inventory_value.view']. Rationale: 12 permission strings from PRD §3 Permission surface matrix + FR-WH-036 force-unlock + V-WH-DASH-001 server-side inventory value gate. Note the canonical prefix is `warehouse.*` (not legacy `wh.*` flagged in the audit) to match the existing `Permission.SETTINGS_*` lowercase-prefix convention. Same conventions as 02-settings T-001/T-002: const object with JSDoc per entry citing the PRD anchor, typed `Permission` union via `(typeof Permission)[keyof typeof Permission]`, and `ALL_WAREHOUSE_PERMISSIONS` declared `as readonly Permission[]`.
out_of_scope: Do not introduce a 4-level matrix permission model.
Do not edit other modules' permission strings.
Do not move permissions into Reference.RolePermissions table (enum is authoritative; the table is only the role→permission assignment mapping).
Do not couple enum strings to specific UI components.

cross_module_dependencies.reason: Settings owns the enum file; this task appends a delimited namespaced group following the T-001 pattern.

ESLint enum-lock guard (`no-direct-permissions-enum-edit`) registered in packages/rbac/.eslintrc.cjs recognises module groups via ALL_<MODULE>_PERMISSIONS exports — this task adds the corresponding ALL_<MODULE>_PERMISSIONS export. (Previously mis-anchored to T-046, which is SET-006 Onboarding Completion; corrected to T-130 per fixer F4.)

acceptance_criteria: All 12 permission strings listed in pipeline_inputs.permission_strings are present in packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts, with JSDoc citing the PRD anchor.
Every new string matches regex ^[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+$ and no duplicates exist across Object.values(Permission); vitest assertion enforces both.
ALL_WAREHOUSE_PERMISSIONS is exported from packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts as `readonly Permission[]` with length === 12; type-check passes.
test_strategy: RED: extend packages/rbac/src/__tests__/permissions.test.ts with presence + regex + uniqueness + array-length assertions for the 12 new strings. Run `pnpm --filter @monopilot/web vitest run packages/rbac/src/__tests__/permissions.test.ts`; expect failure.
GREEN: append the strings + ALL_WAREHOUSE_PERMISSIONS export; re-run vitest; expect pass.
Run `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm lint` after the change; both must pass.
risk_red_lines: Do not introduce a parallel permission model (no per-module *PermissionEnum file).
Do not remove or rename existing strings.
Do not edit permission strings owned by other modules.
Do not couple enum strings to specific UI components or table rows.
